name: jvm-extract
description: Extract J9 JVM runtime, UI framework JARs, and application config for offline reverse engineering — the first step toward custom MMI menu entries
author: dspl1236
scripts: jvm_extract.sh - Copy JVM, JARs, configs, and run string analysis on lsd.jxe
extracts: lsd/lsd.jxe - Main UI application (~25MB J9 JXE blob)
lsd/AppDevelopment.jar - GEM plugin (decompile to understand bundle architecture)
lsd/lsd.sh - JVM startup script (classpath, JVM flags, system properties)
lsd/*.jar - All JARs (DSITracer, texts, myAudi connect, etc.)
j9/ - J9 JVM binary and java.properties
jxe-analysis/ - Extracted strings: bundle config, class names, CAN refs, menu structure
notes: Read-only operation — copies files to SD card, changes nothing on the MMI. Output is ~30-50MB depending on firmware. The jxe-analysis folder contains pre-extracted strings from lsd.jxe highlighting bundle registration, Java class names, CAN/vehicle data references, and menu/screen structure — no decompiler needed for initial reconnaissance. IMPORTANT timing caveat: several binaries under /mnt/ifs-root/usr/apps/ (including MMI3GApplication) become invisible to the shell ~3 minutes after boot. For full results, insert the SD card BEFORE powering on or within the first 90 seconds. The script probes for this and warns in the log if run too late. For a timing-independent alternative, use tools/inflate_ifs.py on a firmware MU update's ifs-root.ifs.
